page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache.

This server type does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities for disk operations. It also does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The processor supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA, though it lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

In benchmark testing, the D48as_v5 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Its single-core performance is also strong in stress-ng, though average in Geekbench. Memory performance is highly competitive, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier, while raw memory bandwidth tests fall in the middle 50% average. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, image processing, and ray tracing benchmarks also rank in the top 25%.

Given its balanced resource allocation and strong multi-core capabilities, the D48as_v5 is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded application servers, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware makes it less suitable for high-I/O databases or heavy deep learning tasks, as evidenced by its poor performance in large-scale (70B) LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores, a 3.525 GHz clock speed, and 192.0 GB of system memory. This configuration delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory scores, though single-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain average. The server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads, but is limited in heavy storage-bound or GPU-accelerated tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 192 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine offering forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and high-tier memory performance for multi-threaded enterprise applications.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-core AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB per vCPU
Strong multi-core CPU and top-tier memory benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU included; Premium Storage capable
Suited for multi-threaded application servers and software compilation
